result.summary: Duo exposes four documented developer-facing HTTP integration surfaces: the Auth API, Admin API, OIDC Auth API, and Duo Single Sign-On as an OAuth/OIDC provider (including MCP-oriented docs); all require Duo-issued application credentials created in the Duo Admin Panel. No public GraphQL API, MCP server endpoint, or first-party general-purpose developer CLI was found on duo.com.
result.description: Duo is Cisco’s cloud identity security platform for multi-factor authentication, single sign-on, device trust, and related access-control features. It is used by organizations to protect application, VPN, desktop, and web access with strong authentication and policy enforcement.

duo_ikey_skey.label: Duo integration key and secret key
duo_ikey_skey.setup: In the [Duo Admin Panel](https://admin.duosecurity.com/), go to **Applications** → **Application Catalog**, add the application/integration you need, and copy the generated `ikey` (integration key) and `skey` (secret key). The [Protecting Applications](https://duo.com/docs/protecting-applications) guide documents creating applications, and the [Getting Started](https://duo.com/docs/getting-started) guide confirms Auth API and Web SDK access.

duo_oidc_client.label: Duo OIDC/OAuth client
duo_oidc_client.setup: In the [Duo Admin Panel](https://admin.duosecurity.com/), create a Duo OIDC/OAuth application for either the [OIDC Auth API / Web SDK](https://duo.com/docs/oauthapi) or [Duo Single Sign-On](https://duo.com/docs/sso-oauth-server). Copy the `client_id`, `client_secret` (for confidential clients), issuer/hostname metadata, and configure the allowed redirect URI(s). The [MCP/OIDC setup guide](https://duo.com/docs/sso-oauth-server-mcp) and [OIDC SSO guide](https://duo.com/docs/sso-oauth-server) document registering clients in Duo.

variables.name: api_hostname
variables.resolveFrom: Auth API application settings in Duo Admin Panel
variables.description: The Duo API hostname for the created application; docs describe a per-application base URL/hostname.

variables.name: subdomain
variables.resolveFrom: Duo Single Sign-On subdomain URL configured in Duo Admin Panel
variables.description: The Duo SSO subdomain; docs say all URLs start with the Issuer under the Metadata tab.
